title: wahlenbergia huttonii
intro: Wahlenbergia huttonii, also known as the New Zealand bluebells or Hutton's wahlenbergia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is a perennial herb that is native to New Zealand and is valued for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Wahlenbergia huttonii is often made into a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
